reviews.review_description:
It was very awesome idea, but implementation was bad.
I lost count of times when i tried to make a gun and failed.
Same with creatures falling through my hand when i try to up them.
There was two or three times i rage quit cause of these problems and other glitches.
If only i had option to switch to controllers...

And it wasn't a problem with hand tracking in general - it worked fine in other titles (such Elixir)

I trust the devs that this game has a good story to it, but I'm too lazy to find out. I got this because of the hand tracking experience it offers, but it really sucks. I honestly don't get what is going on. I forgot what the graphics are like; I haven't played this game in like a year, but I'm guessing they're not the best. I think the best part was the scenery, but that's it. This game would be so much more fun if it had controller tracking. Before you buy, realize that hand tracking can be pretty sketchy. Don't get games where that is their only feature.

Silhouette’s puzzles aren’t very challenging, and the hand tracking controls don’t always work well, but it’s a good deal at $9.99.

The graphics are stylized, good but not spectacular, but the music helps make up for that. 28 Puzzles in all, and will probably take you around 3 hours or so.

For a full review, search YouTube for 6DOF Reviews Silhouette :)

reviews.review_title: We need more original games like this!
reviews.review_description: Brilliant and original. Kudos to the devs. We truly need more games that pushes the capability of this awesome device. Hope to see more updates to this and sequels.

developer_response.body: Thank you so much for your kind words, we're thrilled to hear you like it!

reviews.review_title: Who doesn't like to play with Shadows
reviews.review_description: Cute little indie puzzler with the bonus of hand-tracking controls to make things more interesting.

developer_response.body: Thank you so much for taking the time to review! 👐

reviews.review_description: Hmm I think the thought is good. But maybe quest is still not prepared for a hand tracking game. The experience is a little struggling.

developer_response.body: Hey hey 👋 Hand tracking is indeed a new technology that first and foremost requires even and bright lighting, but also benefits greatly from the latest generation headsets and improved sensors. If you are on a Quest 2, we would be honored to hear your impressions and feedback if you should upgrade to a later model.

reviews.review_description: Kudos for the innovative idea. However, hand tracking is imprecise and frustrating. It ruins the immersion.

developer_response.body: Thanks for the feedback! When it comes to hand tracking in Silhouette we recommend trying different locations in your house (good lighting conditions and evenly lit rooms are highly recommended). Also try to avoid moving your hands too quickly and try to make sure your hands are visible to the headset cameras. We hope your experience improves after some rearranging! :)

reviews.review_description: A chill, clever game actually built around the hand tracking functions, with a charming, meditative soundtrack and the cutest character noises. Works even better on the Quest 3. A great way to discover hand tracking in VR.

developer_response.body: Thank you so much for dropping us a review! 🫶

reviews.review_description:
Silhouette is a fun adventure puzzle game, with a unique way to play it. You will only have hand tracking in this game, and must use your hands to create shadows to solve unique puzzles. This is something that has to be experienced in VR to get the full feel of the game. We will be exploring an island and helping cute little shadow creatures from point A to B. The game features 28 different puzzles to discover, each with multiple ways to solve. You must use your hands to cast shadows and manipulate shadows and object to complete each puzzle. This is challenging and a ton of fun. I have never experienced such good hand tracking before and such a unique way of using it.

The world you are in is beautiful and relaxing. You do not feel any pressure solving the puzzles or exploring. Its very zenfull, do at your on pace. If you love puzzle games this is a very clever puzzle game, with a new way to interact with the puzzles. This is a must experience in VR game to truly appreciate what it has to offer. 

Watch my preview here,
https://youtu.be/x9Pxok14vPY

reviews.review_title: Love it! Hand tracking is awesome!
reviews.review_description:
First off im a bit of a puzzle nut and love puzzles on my quest. This game definitely scratched that itch.

the environments are beautiful and add to the immersion whilst providing a relaxing ambience. 

I love that I can sit in my chair and not have to worry about controllers. The hand tracking is some of the most inventive and well executed I have seen on the quest.

The character you save is both cute and at times frustrating until you get that light bulb moment.

Check out my gameplay here 

https://youtu.be/-zQ-ifbvg4o

developer_response.body: Thanks a lot for your thorough review and the four stars. Great to hear you're enjoying your time with the Shadowies! :)

reviews.review_description: I really love this game! The puzzles are challenging and it's a great sense  of accomplishment every time I complete one. I feel like both sides of my brain are getting a workout!  The hand tracking is excellent. Something other games should aspire to.   I love the little "bird." I'll be sorry when I'm done and I hope there is either an update with more challenges or a second edition. Highly recommend.

developer_response.body: Thank you SO much for the positivity and five stars, Kate.Robbie, we appreciate it a lot! :)

reviews.review_description: Hand tracking shadow puzzles in a colorful world to stimulate the mind and exercise your hand movements. It also has a calming zen effect. I strongly recommend. If developers can continue to support future updates of Meta hand tracking modules that would be awesome.

developer_response.body:
Hey there Shayor, thank you so much for the review! Updates and improvements to the handtracking will be coming soon(tm) :)

Edit: The game was just updated (version 1.2), let us know if you have a smoother experience!

reviews.review_description: After the fifth game it glitches out

developer_response.body: Hey Nerds, we're here to help. Could you please describe your issue in a bit more detail, or send over an email to support@beyondframes.com?

reviews.review_description:
Silhouette had me nostalgic for old puzzle games like Lemmings. The handtracking adds a new wrinkle. This may have just been my experience, but I had issues with handtracking and using the finger gun with my right hand was problematic.

Experienced puzzle gamers will breeze through this, like I did, in roughly three hours. For $10, it's tremendous value for a 3 VR experience. I've paid much more for much shorter. 

There is no storyline in this game, so if you want a story puzzler closer to Shadow Point or the I Expect You To Die games, skip this one. It's more along the lines of Puzzling Places or Kartoffl. The puzzle IS the game.

My full review: https://youtu.be/DLEZjY5OBCQ
This game is a waste of money don’t spend a penny on this game the puzzles are to easy and the hand tracking is garbage

developer_response.body:
Hi Y03yVR,

We're sorry you had issues with the hand tracking. As a new and evolving technology, we are constantly learning of how to implement it to the best of our abilities. Were there any specific gestures or situations that caused issues?

In general, we would recomend trying different spaces in your house to see if it improves, a well and even-lit room and making sure your hands are visible to the headset cameras helps a great deal.

reviews.review_description:
VR needs many more games like this. 
Imaginative ones. Games that can only exist in VR.

For me, this game shows the potential of both VR as a medium and the reward we will all reap by having faith in it’s future.
Meta is in for a glorious year! Quest 3 and beautiful games like this.

A little gem of a game with a great concept and gameplay, and wonderful music.

You have to guide adorable little shadow beings (the Shadowies) through increasingly complex puzzles and, boy, never have I felt so guilty for accidentally throwing virtual creatures into the void. They are incredibly cute. Getting to hold them in the palm of your hand also makes you uniquely invested in them.

The puzzles - hand tracking based - involve using your hands' shadows to help the Shadowies reach the exit, by creating bridges for them, carrying them up, or manipulating the shadow environment. It's fun, challenging and refreshing. More complex mechanics are added as you progress, which keeps things interesting. 

Between the endearing little creatures, the beautiful music and the pleasant scenery, the game ends up both extremely immersive and fun, as well as relaxing (except when you get uniquely invested in adorable shadow beings and then accidentally drop them, of course).

Definitely worth playing to experience it firsthand, especially at this price.

developer_response.body: Thank you for a lovely review (and the five stars), mostlypixels! We're thrilled to hear you like Silhouette so much! <3

reviews.review_title: This is what VR is all about!
reviews.review_description:
Such an immersive experience that is clever with hand tracking and fun puzzles to get lost in your own world for hours on end. 

I highly recommend giving it a try to get a relaxed experience and have fun at the same time. 

Great job to the developers for coming out with such an intuitive game.

I loved the idea of hand tracking and the concept of the game is creative.  However it quickly loses its charm once it stops recognizing hand gestures or even loses ability to recognize your whole hand.  I am stuck on a level the requires the shadow gun, however it barely registers the gesture to form the gun and when it does, the gun pops into existence only to disappear immediately.  I can hear the gun clicking on and off when my hands aren't even in the field of vision, but when I want to access it....not happening. There is no other way to pass the level, so it looks like I am done with this game.  In addition, the hand shadows will randomly disappear even though the effects still work (i.e. I can move my hand around to interact with aspects of the puzzle, but without a visible shadow, I can't track where my hand is in relation to the puzzle).  Once again, the first few puzzles were fun and entertaining, as you progress in the game, they get more complex and handtracking just isn't up to par.  I am currently playing this game on the Meta Quest 2 device.  There have been issues throughout the game, but by far Level 22 has been the most frustrating with trying to use the gun.

developer_response.body:
[Update 3]
We've patched the game for the holidays with further tweaking to the gun gesture!

[Update 2]
Thank you for clarifying your device! The team is working on further improvements to the gun gesture, keep an eye out for an update notification in the near future.
[Original response]
Hey there, thank you for reporting the issues you are experiencing with the shooting-gesture, we will look at improving it further in the next update! Might we ask which version of the Quest hardware you are currently playing on? (You can update your review and we will see the additional information).

reviews.review_title: It was cute otherwise kinda awful
reviews.review_description: All that I got from the experience was an extremely glitchy mess where the characters fell through my hand and the gun function rarely read my hands which might just be my oculus so keep it in mind, but I have other games that I love playing with hand tracking so it might just be the game. On top of that there is no hight adjuster so if you are short like me have a stool prepared to finish some of the puzzles. Besides that I liked the world design and music, these could make a good meditation app if the puzzles didn't make me so mad. If you think getting angry over puzzles that are impossible to do with the hand tracking is worth just the look of a game this game may be for you.

developer_response.body: Hey there and thank you for your thoughts on Silhouette! The latest version should’ve improved the gun gesture recognition but will will continue to see if we can improve. You should however have a height adjust option in the menu, please let us know here or via our Discord if you can’t find it!
